_comment: Default RSS sources seeded into the source registry on first run. Add or remove entries here to change what ships with a fresh install. Sources can also be enabled/disabled at runtime through the Analytics tab without editing this file.

sources.name: Yahoo Finance
sources.notes: Aggregates Reuters, AP, Motley Fool, and Benzinga for general market news. Per-ticker feeds are also built dynamically for each symbol you track.

sources.name: PR Newswire
sources.notes: Official press releases from public companies. High signal for earnings announcements, executive changes, M&A, and product launches.

sources.name: MarketWatch
sources.notes: Real-time market headlines from Dow Jones.

sources.name: SEC EDGAR
sources.notes: 8-K material event filings directly from the SEC. The highest-authority source for earnings, executive changes, and regulatory actions.

sources.name: Wall St Journal
sources.notes: WSJ markets feed. Paywalled full text but headlines are surfaced via RSS.

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.notes: Bloomberg markets feed. Paywalled — headline and lede only. Low pass rate expected.

sources.name: Benzinga
sources.notes: Fast-moving market news, analyst calls, and price target changes.

sources.name: Seeking Alpha
sources.notes: Opinion-heavy; most articles will be classified General News and filtered. Useful for tracking market sentiment.

sources.name: CoinTelegraph
sources.notes: Crypto-focused news. Enable if you track Bitcoin, Ethereum, or crypto macro themes.

sources.name: OilPrice
sources.notes: Commodities and energy sector focus. Enable if you track oil, gas, or energy equities.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.notes: General world news. Useful for geopolitical and macro themes.

sources.name: DW News
sources.notes: German public broadcaster. Good European macro and geopolitical coverage.

sources.name: South China Morning Post
sources.notes: Asia-Pacific business and geopolitical coverage. Relevant for China-exposed equities.
